notes:
Sao usually means “why?” in a casual way. It’s shorter and more natural in speech than tại sao.

It goes at the beginning of a question, before the subject.  

It can also appear at the end of a sentence as a softer “why” (… là sao?).

Very common second meaning: sao = star (on the sky or as a symbol). Think: ngôi sao = star (celebrity or literal star).
exampleSentences: Em không hiểu sao anh lại buồn hôm nay.
Chị có thể nói cho em biết sao mình phải đi sớm không?
Sao anh không trả lời điện thoại của em tối qua?

definitions.vi: Dùng để hỏi về lý do hoặc nguyên nhân.
definitions.en: Used to ask about a reason or cause.
definitions.es: Se usa para preguntar sobre una razón o causa.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I don't understand why you are sad today.
Can you tell me why we have to leave early?
Why didn't you answer my phone call last night?
notes_md:
**Sao** usually means **“why?”** in a casual way. It’s shorter and more natural in speech than *tại sao*.

It goes at the **beginning of a question**, before the subject.  

It can also appear at the **end of a sentence** as a softer “why” (… là sao?).

Very common second meaning: **sao = star** (on the sky or as a symbol). Think: *ngôi sao* = star (celebrity or literal star).
